name: Black Bean Chili with Red Onion Salsa
recipeIngredient: Crushed red pepper or Cayenne to taste
recipeInstructions:
Cheddar cheese - grated
Monterey jack cheese - grated
Sour cream - for topping
RED ONION SALSA:
2 cups Red onion, chopped
1/2 cup Fresh cilantro, minced
2 cups Fresh tomatoes, minced
1/2 teaspoon Salt (optional)
1 cup Parsley, minced
Black pepper to taste
Soak beans in plenty of water for several hours or overnight. Drain the soaking water and cook in fresh boiling water, partly covered, until tender (1-1/2 hours). Check water level during cooking; add more as necessary. Transfer cooked beans to a large kettle or saucepan. Include about 2-3 cups of their cooking liquid.
In a heavy skillet, sauté garlic, seasonings, lime juice, and bell peppers in olive oil over medium-low heat until the peppers are tender (10-15 minutes). Add the sautéed mixture to the cooked beans, along with tomato puree and minced green chilies. Simmer uncovered over very low heat, stirring occasionally, for about 45 minutes. Make the salsa during this time. Serve topped with Red Onion Salsa and, if desired, grated cheese and sour cream. For a finer consistency, give the mixture a few whirrs in a food processor or blender. Yield: 6-8 servings.
